Our threat intelligence detected 1 billion resident records for sell in the dark web, including name, address, national id, mobile, police and medical records from one asian country. Likely due to a bug in an Elastic Search deployment by a gov agency. This has impact on ...

Gigantic civilian data leak if confirmed: A hacker is selling an alleged Shanghai police data leak containing 1 billion Chinese nationals' names, home addresses, ID #, phone #, criminal records, etc. Hacker says it's from an Aliyun (Alibaba) private cloud server. https://twitter.com/...

A hacker is selling an alleged 1 billion Chinese citizens' information stolen from Shanghai police. @rachelliang5602 & I downloaded the sample the hacker provided and called dozens of people listed. Nine picked up & confirmed exactly what the data said. https://www.wsj.com/...

Apparently, this exploit happened because the gov developer wrote a tech blog on CSDN and accidentally included the credentials. 1 billion records of private citizens' data. 😭 https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

If you're not following this, you should be: word on the social media street is that China's police force (MPS - Shanghai) database was hacked, with the personal information and case records of 1 billion citizens, and the records are for sale on Telegram - 23TB of data. 1/7 https://twitter.com/...

hacker detection/prevention measures, mobile numbers used for account take overs, etc. It is important for all platforms to enhance their security measures in this area. @Binance has already stepped up verifications for users potentially affected. Stay #SAFU. 🙏
CZ, founder and CEO of Binance, talked about the leak here: https://twitter.com/... I wonder if Binance bought or would buy the leaked data. https://twitter.com/...

This is pretty sensational if true. I spoke to @_KarenHao yesterday and per her thread below, she'd reached out to individuals in the dump and they'd confirmed the accuracy of the data. This isn't data aggregator stuff either, it's police reports so very unique data. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

This is what will happen with collected data from police and government. It'll be stolen. The more we're surveilled for the sake of fighting terrorism and money laundering, the more data will be stolen or sold, which will be used to attack us. #privacy https://www.bloomberg.com/...

At this point, it's impossible to confirm the scale of the data leak, but five of the people who picked up verified all of the case details listed with their name — information that would would be difficult to obtain from any source other than the police.
Shanghai police database with one billion PRC residents info got hacked in the biggest cybersecurity breach in China's history. https://www.bloomberg.com/... by @_szheng https://twitter.com/...

CZ: detected 1 billion resident records for sell in the dark web, including name, address, national id, mobile, police and medical records (may China), likely due to a bug in an Elastic Search deployment by gov. Binance has stepped up verifications for users potentially affected. https://twitter.com/...
The sample contains individuals' personal names, national ID numbers, phone numbers, birthdays and birthplaces, as well as detailed summaries of crimes and incidents they had reported to the police.

The other four confirmed basic information like their names before hanging up. One man, upon hearing why we had his information, sighed in resignation: “We are all running naked,” he said, using popular Chinese slang for a lack of privacy.
10 BTC for personal records belonging to more than 1 billion Chinese citizens. Seems unreal... https://breached.to/... https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...
If this is accurate, wow, I mean... just wow 😮 https://twitter.com/...

A hacker claims to have obtained a trove of personal information from the Shanghai police on 1 billion Chinese citizens, and has offered to sell the 23 terabytes of data — including names, addresses, national ID numbers and phone numbers — for 10 bitcoin. https://www.reuters.com/...

A hacker is selling billions of records of Chinese data stolen from police in Shanghai, at least some of which is legitimate. If confirmed, it would mark one of history's largest leaks of personal data. https://www.wsj.com/...

A vast trove of data on Chinese citizens allegedly siphoned from a police database, some of which checks out as legitimate, is being offered for sale by an anonymous hacker or hacking group. https://www.wsj.com/...

A surveillance state is just a helpful dataset for your adversaries to inevitably obtain. They probably all had access already, but all of the intelligence agencies are combing through this to find new espionage targets. https://twitter.com/...
A big wake up call for China. Sad for the victims. https://twitter.com/...

1/ Possibly the most significant #databreach in history has occurred with over 1 Billion residents' information exposed. This likely happened due to an improper setup of a popular tool made by @elastic. The database was left vulnerable online for any malicious user to exploit. https://twitter.com/...
And EU thinks their upcoming database mapping wallet addresses to name & street address isn't going to leak to criminals? https://twitter.com/...
Hacker(s) wants 10 bitcoins for over 23 terabytes of data that has been stolen on around 1 billion Chinese citizens https://www.bloomberg.com/...

According to Mark Gurman, the Apple Watch Series 8 will be able to detect if a user has a fever using the new built-in body temperature sensor. The watch won't give users a direct reading, instead, it will alert a user if it believes they have a fever https://twitter.com/...

I'm not at all disappointed. Heart rate + body temp for AirPods seemed weird in the first place. Any implementation like that would have to compliment the Apple Watch, not try and replace it. Plus, just give us better quality with no random popping and better reliability for ANC. https://twitter.com/...

I am all for Apple making the Apple Watch the ultimate health device, but it needs better battery life first. I'm not sure how they expect to jam-pack even more sensors and capabilities into the watch when its battery life is already subpar to what's on the market. https://twitter.com/...
Apple Watch Series 8 could get the biggest screen Apple ever put on a wrist https://www.imore.com/...

Apple Watch “Explorer Edition” rumors are really coming together for me. There's no way I can prove the rumors about a flat design, a flat display, a larger display, and a third, “rugged” model are all about the same device - but it's starting to seem like an obvious conclusion. https://twitter.com/...

A report by Bloomberg reveals that the Apple Watch Series 8 will come with a body temperature sensor. If it believes you have a fever, it will recommend that you talk to your doctor. https://soyacincau.com/...

Time-series health data accumulated from users of Apple Watch is going to be a veritable gold mine that could be sold to 3rd party clients engaged in epidemiological and Real World Data/Evidence (RWD/E) research. Hopefully Apple will not drop the ball on this data opportunity. https://twitter.com/...

.@markgurman reports @Apple's initiative to develop a modem chip to replace @Qualcomm has failed due to thermal issues. Will this affect iPhone integration of @Globalstar sat messaging? $QCOM supports $GSAT's frequency but only for terrestrial use (n53). https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Apologies for the temporary interruption to our feed. We will conduct a full investigation and learn from this incident. Thanks for following us and normal service will now resume.

We are aware of a breach of the Army's Twitter and YouTube accounts and an investigation is underway. The Army takes information security extremely seriously and is resolving the issue. Until their investigation is complete it would be inappropriate to comment further.

The breach of the Army's Twitter and YouTube accounts that occurred earlier today has been resolved and an investigation is underway. The Army takes information security extremely seriously and until their investigation is complete it would be inappropriate to comment further.
This looks serious. I hope the results of the investigation and actions taken will be shared appropriately. https://twitter.com/...

Meanwhile, over at YouTube, the British Army's account has been rebranded to resemble ARK Invest and used to play those fake “double-your-money” Bitcoin/Ether scams with the Elon Musk/Jack Dorsey videos. https://twitter.com/...

Anyhow @BritishArmy's accounts got pwned, it already was good for one thing: to show that Google and Twitter keeps not only not giving a fuck about “random” accounts' getting pwned / taken over by idiots/scammers/etc, but not even about such “important” accounts... https://twitter.com/...

This is not exactly encouraging in volatile times, especially when the British Army also has its 77th Brigade doing covert strategic communication operations intended to steer public opinion. It was supposed to be our designated adversary who were causing information confusion... https://twitter.com/...
Military grade = weak password without 2FA? 😜 https://twitter.com/...

“Sir, I'm afraid our Twitter account has been hacked and used to post videos about cryptocurrency and NFTs featuring Elon Musk” What a moment that must have been. It's a tragedy we'll never get to see a recording of that conversation. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...
We take information security extremely seriously, after something bad has happened. https://twitter.com/...

I can't help but think this apology would have been very different had the temporary ability to impersonate the British Army been used to cause damage or sow confusion, in any of the many ways that could have been done, rather than merely promote an NFT. https://twitter.com/...

This is pretty serious, really. Surely the Defence of the Realm has to mean more than just tanks and guns. We live in a digital world. For our military to lose control of their Twitter feed strikes as a real failure of their digital capacity. A real one. Especially given this. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Ummmm #BritishArmy not using 2 factor authentication?🤔 Tweets posted from hacked acct wr funny to say the least. 😃 https://twitter.com/...

This is hilarious. The British Army Twitter was hacked not by the Russians but by an NFT scam. Or maybe it's a fabulous double bluff by the Russians? Either way does not give one confidence. https://www.reuters.com/...

Lending platform Celsius has laid off about 150 employees, including those in Israel, according to Israeli media Calcalistech. The layoffs represent about a quarter of Celsius' total workforce. Many of Celsius' businesses are run by third-party platforms. https://www.calcalistech.com/ ...

Celsius continues to reassure customers that everything is totally fine. “Our relationship with the community and our clients has been a source of pride for all team members at Celsius...” https://blog.celsius.network/ ...
“pursuing strategic transactions as well as a restructuring of our liabilities” Translation: it can't pay its debts and is desperately seeking a buyer. https://twitter.com/...
“Everyone's been left in the dark here, nobody knows exactly what's going on with them,” said one Celsius customer. “Are they insolvent or simply illiquid?” By @Vlajournaliste https://www.wsj.com/...
When the self-proclaimed Crypto King can't withdraw his funds, what does he do? Sells his watches to buy more crypto. 🤌🤌🤌 https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...
It sure sounds like Celsius retail customers will be lucky to get any of their tokens back. https://blog.celsius.network/ ...

4/ Arthur Hayes Some conclusions of 3AC implosion, how they started, how this happened. Also, conclusion that centralized lenders failed en masse, while their decentralized counterparts liquidated collateral and operated with no hiccups. https://entrepreneurshandbook.co/ ...
Read!! How 2AC blew a whale shark-sized hole in crypto markets with reputation based leverage across centralized lending platforms https://entrepreneurshandbook.co/ ...

Voyager has halted withdrawals, joining Celsius in freezing customer deposits. Also reveals that almost 60% of the its outstanding crypto loans are to Three Arrows Capital, which is in liquidation https://www.prnewswire.com/...
This is crazy. Lenders like Voyager concentrating this much of their loan portfolio with just 1-2 parties. What could possibly go wrong 🙄 https://www.prnewswire.com/... https://twitter.com/...

“When you remove trust from the equation and rely purely on transparent lending standards executed by impartial computer code, you get a better outcome. This is the lesson to be learned” -@CryptoHayes's essay on 3AC & ecosystem is my best read this week! https://entrepreneurshandbook.co/ ...

“Both centralised and decentralised lending companies / platforms had exposure to 3AC — and only the players in one of these two markets went belly up. The centralised lenders failed en masse, while their decentralised counterparts...” ~ @CryptoHayes https://entrepreneurshandbook.co/ ...

Another excellent blog by @CryptoHayes - despite the market carnage, it has been encouraging to see how well DeFi protocols fared. I hope that regulators and the public see how DeFi can lead to better outcomes for lenders, borrowers, and investors. https://entrepreneurshandbook.co/ ... https://twitter.com/...

“Both centralised & decentralised lending companies/ platforms had exposure to 3AC - & only the players in one of these two markets went belly up.” Great analysis of the Three Arrows Capital (3AC) blowup and how decentralized platforms faired much better. https://cryptohayes.medium.com/ ...

This was a good read on how crypto broke, but I'm still left wondering why people are so excited about lending that requires 150%+ collateral. Is the TAM huge for this? Does crypto expand the market or materially lower costs? DeFi survived, so what? https://entrepreneurshandbook.co/ ... https://twitter.com/...

Amazon has launched its first micromobility hub, bringing deliveries 📦 to customers with zero emissions, as part of its mission of being net-zero carbon by 2040. Find out more 👇 https://www.aboutamazon.eu/...

EBikes cut through traffic like butter, speeding up deliveries. Amazon are making the switch starting in Hackney, which is leading the way on cycling infrastructure. I'm hoping we won't be too far behind in Oxfordshire 🚲💷 https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

“Amazon's move comes as window cleaners, milk and beer deliverers, plumbers and DJs all get on their bikes” #Amazon: e-cargo bikes to replace thousands of van deliveries in London | Amazon | The Guardian https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

This would have never happened without London's new Low Traffic Neighbourhoods. Fitting that Hackney - where I delievered the largest number of LTNs in the U.K - should be the beneficiary of the new jobs associated with this micromobility hub. https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

I'm incredibly proud that Hackney has had a huge part in making this happen. This new trial in Hackney with Amazon will reduce tonnes of transport emissions and traffic, and help support our efforts to tackle the climate emergency✌🏾🌍 https://www.theguardian.com/ ...
E-cargo bikes are an amazing way to speed up deliveries, while cutting down on carbon emissions! https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

In the last five years the number of vans in the UK has risen by 650,000 (!) but this is what happens if you create the conditions. Kudos @mayorofhackney @metecoban92 👏👏👏https:// www.theguardian.com/...
Some interesting news from @amazon today - hope this is a meaningful programme that will be further expanded and not just tokenistic greenwashing 🤨 https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

As gas ⛽️ prices continue to rise we're likely going to see a lot more innovation in the supply chain, from accelerated electrification of delivery vehicles to more robust supply chain “mesh” networks able to adapt faster with people, software and delivery https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

I recall ⁦@SammSacks⁩ telling me about four years ago that the proper way to look at cars was as computers on wheels, and that we should expect them to be regulated accordingly. This certainly adds urgency to that process. https://www.wired.com/...

Sun Valley 2022 will discuss inflation, future of streaming video; Tim Cook expected
Who's Who At The Legendary Summer Camp For Billionaires In Sun Valley, Idaho
Tim Cook returning to Sun Valley media conference
“Will Humility Have Set In?” Media Moguls Return to Sun Valley Under Darkening Financial Skies
Apple CEO Tim Cook Among Tech and Media Elite Invited to Sun Valley Conference Later This Week

If the people at this conference decide which social media platforms go mainstream, how they function, and integrate them with intelligence agencies, they function not only as the eyes and ears of big brother but as the thought police and agenda setters as well. (6/7)

“If you have to ask what it costs, you can't really afford to do it.” Inside the secretive world of billionaire jet travel, as hundreds of moguls prepare to descend on small-town Idaho. https://www.nytimes.com/...

Bill Gates, Rupert Murdoch, Tim Cook, Jack Dorsey, Meg Whitman, and Tim Armstrong have all been documented in attendance. There also have been major acquisition deals made there like: BET > Viacom Comcast > NBCUniversal Microsoft > MSNBC Instagram > Facebook (3/7)
The “Runway Tetris” at Sun Valley airport was always a fun summer curiosity during my decade living in Hailey, Idaho. That and bumping into Tim Cook in the coffee shop. https://twitter.com/...

“The secretive and exclusive gathering of media, tech and other power brokers” sure gets a lot of publicity despite being secretive. The Sun Valley gathering of has been nicknamed “summer camp for moguls and the reporters who write about them.” https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/ ...

As someone who lost 6 million fans when Facebook & Twitter removed our pages, this could explain how 2 separate companies banned us on the same day and explain the political bias, selective enforcement, “fact-checking” narrative control, and agenda-driven terms of service. (5/7)

Here's where it gets interesting... The CIA is also a regular attendee at this conference, in fact the one time CIA director, George Tenet, was once the keynote speaker in 2003 and 2005. Former CIA director Gen. David Petraeus has also attended. (4/7)

Machine learning-powered surveillance systems will never eliminate the risk of US school shootings, no matter how expansive and intricate the systems become
New Wired piece. “...at their root, what many of these predictive systems promise is a measure of certainty in situations about which there can be none.” https://www.wired.com/...

The failure is not only in the spurious systems, but in the belief that more data can improve them. https://www.wired.com/... via @wired #tech #surveillance #schools cc: @MiaD @pierrepinna @psb_dc @TamaraMcCleary @ipfconline1 @DrJDrooghaag @enilev

Great story @hypervisible - on school shootings: about how surveillance tech is not a magic safety bullet. In our quest to be safe we have always leapt at any possible solution, inattentive to social costs - and frequently and up unsafe and creating harm. https://www.wired.com/...
And human beings are infinitely susceptible to belief in magic beans and guaranteed outcomes. Barnum was right. https://twitter.com/...

Phenomenal article by @hypervisible that points out surveillance tech has repeatedly and demonstrably not worked in preventing horrible things and it makes no sense to look at its failure and conclude “the solution is more of the thing that doesn't work.” https://www.wired.com/...

Predictive surveillance in #EdTech is terrifying Pay attention “This hyper-surveilled future is likely to significantly increase the violence visited upon Black students, trans students, and now, given the overturning of Roe, students seeking information on sexual health.” https://twitter.com/...

Compelling argument on why we can't predict ourselves out of school shootings w/ more data: “Much like policing itself, every failure of a surveillance or security system most typically results in people calling for more extensive surveillance.” https://twitter.com/...
In case you were not aware, @hypervisible has good opinions. This is why you should read what he has to say about surveillance in schools: https://www.wired.com/...

How El Salvador President Nayib Bukele weaponized bitcoin to whitewash his government's growing authoritarianism on the world stage and spread local propaganda
Is El Salvador's Bitcoin Experiment Authoritarian Propaganda?
This might be at points painful to read, but it's really good and comes from a genuine local voice https://www.nytimes.com/...
“After all, the economic freedom Bitcoin promises is worth nothing to Salvadorans if it's the only freedom we can hope to have”, @raudaz_ en @nytopinion. 👏🏼👏🏼 👏🏼 https://www.nytimes.com/...

PROPAGANDA: Bitcoin solves the problem of cross-border money transfers, cheaply and instantly, especially for remittances by workers in diaspora from poor countries. REALITY: In El Salvador “only 1.5% of remittances went through digital wallets in April” https://www.nytimes.com/...

This @raudaz_ piece doesn't mince words: “It's pretty obvious to anyone who visits any place in El Salvador other than its beaches that Mr. Bukele is not building a techno-utopia; he's building a run-of-the-mill authoritarian state in a tech disguise.” https://www.nytimes.com/...

🇸🇻 Very critical piece on pro-#Bitcoin move in El Salvador: “A poll showed that only 11% of respondents believed the main beneficiaries of the Bitcoin law are the people, while about 80% believed it's either the rich, foreign investors, banks, businesspeople or the government” https://twitter.com/...

Probably the best critique I've read of of #Bitcoin in a long while. While it's exciting (and I sure was excited in ES and at Bukele's party) to have our thesis validated by adoption, we should be very cautious not to be coopted. https://www.nytimes.com/...

This is the most desolating piece I've read on Bukele and Bitcoin. “It's pretty obvious to anyone who visits El Salvador [...] that Mr. Bukele is not building a techno-utopia; he's building a run-of-the-mill authoritarian state in a tech disguise.” https://www.nytimes.com/...

“A large number of the president's messages about Bitcoin are in English because they are designed for Bitcoin believers, not the Salvadoran people, even though the project is funded by taxpayer money.” https://www.nytimes.com/...
In El Salvador, writes @raudaz_, “Bitcoin believers are promoting a product — and lining their pockets — at the expense of our rights and livelihoods.” https://www.nytimes.com/...

“Bitcoiners aren't supposed to trust government. After all, one of Bitcoin's premises is to separate money from the state. But what happens when the enthusiast is the state?” A perspective to consider from Salvadoran investigative journalist @raudaz_ https://www.nytimes.com/...

Some have suggested it's good “to have these crypto influencers reshaping the image of El Salvador before the world, that there's some sort of invaluable rebranding happening by someone paying for a coconut in Bitcoin,” writes @raudaz_. “It's a mirage.” https://www.nytimes.com/...

“Bukele is not building a techno-utopia; he's building a run-of-the-mill authoritarian state in a tech disguise.” — Nelson Rauda on the Bitcoin bros self-serving adulation of Bukele. https://twitter.com/...

Bitcoiners would do well to remember that when they cheer for President Nayib Bukele of El Salvador, “they're not ushering in the technology of the future; they're enabling a regime that's violating the human rights of its citizens,” writes @raudaz_. https://www.nytimes.com/...

“Bitcoin is the regime's currency, primarily designed for foreign crypto enthusiasts,” writes @raudaz_. “A large number of the president's messages about Bitcoin are in English because they are designed for Bitcoin believers, not the Salvadoran people.” https://www.nytimes.com/... https://twitter.com/...

If you still think Bitcoin is a net positive for human rights in developing nations, you're either lying to yourself or complicit with the autocrats or both. And the number of western elites (investors, CEOs, etc) in denial about this is maddening. https://www.nytimes.com/...
“Over the past three months, the government has used a state of emergency to imprison almost 40,000 people, often without defense,” writes @raudaz_ from El Salvador. https://www.nytimes.com/...

Salvadoran journalist from ⁦@elfaroenglish⁩: “It's obvious to anyone who visits any place in El Salvador other than its beaches that Mr. Bukele is not building a techno-utopia; he's building a run-of-the-mill authoritarian state in a tech disguise” https://www.nytimes.com/...
.@raudaz_ is correct: what was sold as banking for the unbanked was really a hail mary to finance a deeply indebted authoritarian state with worthless bonds https://www.nytimes.com/...
⁦@raudaz_⁩ breaks down what is happening in El Salvador w/Bitcoin. Remember this name: Nelson Rauda Zablah. You will be hearing a lot more of it. https://www.nytimes.com/...
Weird, it's the same in the US, too. https://www.nytimes.com/...
If you want a picture of the future, imagine a boot stamping on a human face — forever — as the boot tweets incessantly about the liberatory nature of Bitcoin https://nytimes.com/... by @raudaz_
One for the “crypto will help poor people in corrupt/authoritarian countries” crowd. Crypto is part of corruption and authoritarianism. https://www.nytimes.com/...
In El Salvador, Pres. Bukele is not building a crypto-utopia, @raudaz_ says. “He's building a run-of-the-mill authoritarian state in a tech disguise.” https://www.nytimes.com/...

When Bitcoiners cheer for President Bukele, they're not ushering in the technology of the future; they're enabling a regime that's violating the human rights of its citizens. My column for the @nytimes https://www.nytimes.com/...

A HackerOne Employee Stole Vulnerability Reports From Security Researchers
Daily Drop (182)  —  Sunday, July 03, 2022 // (IG): BB //Weekly Sponsor: Zanes Hand Made (leather works)
How Bug Bounty Platform HackerOne Handled Its Own ‘Internal Threat’ Actor

HackerOne discloses security incident. The company says that a “then-employee had improperly accessed security reports for personal gain” and shared details about a vulnerability outside the platform https://hackerone.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Frontrunning their own bug bounty is not something I expected to see... https://twitter.com/...

Still waiting for one that will sell reports as zero-days to threat actors. With bug bounty platforms getting increasingly popular, it's only a matter of time. And it will be way harder to detect and to trace than it was here. https://twitter.com/...
Malicious insiders, a nightmare for a company. https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/ ... #infosec

We've been criticized for not having a 3rd party run our bug bounty program but this is exactly why we run it ourselves. Resubmitting bugs for the bounty is the least bad thing that can happen. Worst case, the stolen bugs are sold to more serious hackers or directly exploited. https://twitter.com/...
Imagine submitting a bug report, watching it sit pending review to be closed as a dupe for someone to take your entire bug report and collect your bounty. https://twitter.com/...

If DoorDash allowed drivers to query customer details of everyone signed up, not just their active order, people would lose their minds. But bug bounty employees accessing critical vulns of customers they aren't even working on? Just a policy stopping it, no technical controls https://twitter.com/...

Crazy. I always made jokes about this and finally has happened. Kudos to hackerone for their work on identifying this quickly. Makes me wonder about the scenarios that don't get caught. This is for sure a type of threat that all companies with BB programs need to watch https://twitter.com/...
Kinda interesting - somebody mentioned security researchers selling their bug bounty exploits on dark web in parallel, but maybe it was this. https://hackerone.com/...

Insider threats affect every business. Transparency is praiseworthy. Yet conveniently forgetting that “policy & contractual” controls are not the same as technical access controls every time something comes up around triage access abuse is its own open bug requiring resolution. https://twitter.com/...

Another example of the danger of insider threats. Company identified the culprit only when a customer received from the rogue employee a bug report similar to one that had been submitted through HackerOne on the same vuln. https://twitter.com/...

This might be the first public headline of something like this that I've seen but there are many a large companies without proper authn/authz on their bug databases and not the first stolen / resold bugs. https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/ ...

Yea, we have been saying this for years. Marking a report critical then dropping it to informational, and then marking it as a duplicate and not letting you see the duplicate...I've been saying @FBI needs to investigate @Hacker0x01 for a long time. https://twitter.com/...
